data.title: NCR Atleos wins ATMIA's Peter Kulik Innovation Award for AI-Assisted Service Management Platform, earning 2 additional ATMmy honors.
data.description: Atlanta-based company NCR Atleos Corporation, listed on the New York Stock Exchange as NATL, was recently awarded the ATM Industry Association (ATMIA) Peter Kulik Innovation Award. The award was given in recognition of their AI-assisted ATM service resolution intelligence platform. This platform has been acknowledged for its ability to improve ATM availability, enhance customer experience, and advance the significance of ATMs in the global financial services sector. This recognition highlights Atleos' commitment to providing innovative solutions for financial institutions, retailers, and consumers, demonstrating their leadership in expanding self-service financial access.

data.description: Landsbankinn has announced the cancellation of its plan to offer covered bonds in February (week 7) as per the issue calendar.

data.title: Iron Mountain Inc (NYSE: IRM) reports Q4 results beating estimates with adjusted earnings at 61 cents per share and revenue at $1.843 billion, driven by storage rental and service revenue growth. Adjusted EBITDA margin remains steady at 38.3%. Storage rental revenue up 13% to $1.06 billion, service revenue surges 22% to $782 million. Global RIM revenue rises to $1.37 billion and Data Center revenue climbs to $237 million.
data.description: Iron Mountain Inc (NYSE: IRM) released its fourth-quarter earnings report on Thursday, surpassing expectations. The company recorded adjusted earnings of 61 cents per share, up from 50 cents the previous year, beating the estimated 59 cents. Adjusted funds from operations (AFFO) per share also saw an increase to $1.44 from $1.24 in the previous year. Revenue stood at $1.843 billion, exceeding the forecasted $1.803 billion, driven by robust storage rental and service revenue. The adjusted EBITDA margin held steady at 38.3%, with operating income reaching $341.04 million. Storage rental revenue experienced a 13% growth to $1.06 billion, while service revenue surged by 22% to $782 million. In terms of segments, Global Records and Information Management (RIM) revenue grew to $1.37 billion from $1.26 billion, while Global Data Center revenue increased to $237 million.

data.title: IPG Photonics stock rises 12% after beating Q4 forecasts with $274.47 million in net sales, $100 million buyback announced.
data.description: IPG Photonics Corp. saw its stock (NASDAQ: IPGP) rise following the release of fourth-quarter results that outperformed analyst expectations, marking a new 52-week high. The stock's surge was fueled by high short interest, with 3.245 million shares held short, accounting for 27.11% of the publicly traded float, indicating significant bearish sentiment. The company reported adjusted earnings of 46 cents per share, surpassing the consensus estimate of 20 cents. Net sales grew by 17% year over year to $274.47 million, exceeding the projected $249.45 million, driven by strong performance in materials processing, medical, and advanced applications. Materials processing, representing 85% of revenue, saw a 17% increase, led by welding, marking, additive manufacturing, and cleaning demand. Other applications also grew by 15%. Emerging growth products contributed 54% of revenue, up from 52% previously.

data.title: Bitcoin may drop to $50K, Ethereum to $1,400 before rebound says Standard Chartered; predicts $100K BTC and $4,000 ETH by 2026.
data.description: Financial institution Standard Chartered has forecasted that Bitcoin is poised to reach $100,000 and Ethereum will surge to $4,000 by the conclusion of 2026. However, before these significant increases, the institution anticipates further declines in the value of these cryptocurrencies.
